tweets.title: R to @sidhant: Full comments: The Security Council has yet again witnessed a brazen attempt by Pakistan today to misuse this august platform for its divisive agenda. The Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir has always been, is, and will remain an integral and inalienable part of India. This is the constitutional and legal reality that Pakistan deliberately chooses to ignore. The only outstanding issue with regard to Jammu and Kashmir is the naked aggression and illegal occupation of India’s sovereign territory by Pakistan.The ongoing developments in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir fully expose the true nature of the Pakistani state. The world is witness to the continued suppression of basic rights, freedom of expression and association and stifling of fundamental freedoms of Kashmiris in Pakistan Occupied Kashmir. These are not isolated instances, but part of a larger design by the Pakistani establishment in Pakistan Occupied Kashmir for close to eight decades. This pattern extends to other regions of Pakistan that also face repression. Incarceration of leaders including a former Prime Minister, electoral outlawing of the main opposition party, a constitutional coup by the military through the 27th Amendment, and other such actions have decimated any semblance of democracy. Pakistan would do well to look inward and address the real issues in the country rather than resorting to fabricated narrative construction with religious imagery aimed at scoring domestic brownie points and misleading the wider UN membership. While similar developments in other countries are excoriated by the United Nations, such repression and developments in Pakistan do not receive the attention they deserve.
tweets.content:
Full comments:

The Security Council has yet again witnessed a brazen attempt by Pakistan today to misuse this august platform for its divisive agenda. The Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir has always been, is, and will remain an integral and inalienable part of India. This is the constitutional and legal reality that Pakistan deliberately chooses to ignore. The only outstanding issue with regard to Jammu and Kashmir is the naked aggression and illegal occupation of India’s sovereign territory by Pakistan.The ongoing developments in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir fully expose the true nature of the Pakistani state. The world is witness to the continued suppression of basic rights, freedom of expression and association and stifling of fundamental freedoms of Kashmiris in Pakistan Occupied Kashmir. These are not isolated instances, but part of a larger design by the Pakistani establishment in Pakistan Occupied Kashmir for close to eight decades. This pattern extends to other regions of Pakistan that also face repression. Incarceration of leaders including a former Prime Minister, electoral outlawing of the main opposition party, a constitutional coup by the military through the 27th Amendment, and other such actions have decimated any semblance of democracy. Pakistan would do well to look inward and address the real issues in the country rather than resorting to fabricated narrative construction with religious imagery aimed at scoring domestic brownie points and misleading the wider UN membership. While similar developments in other countries are excoriated by the United Nations, such repression and developments in Pakistan do not receive the attention they deserve.
tweets.author: @sidhant

tweets.content:
USTR Jamison Greer will make an announcement on tariffs issue later today

Sidhant Sibal (@sidhant)

Temporary 10% global US tariff (Section 122 surcharge) is set to expire tomm. Without replacement measures, many exports would face only standard Most Favored Nation (MFN) tariffs, though US could roll out new tariff regime.

tweets.content:
🚨🇵🇰🪖💥 Just In:

Explosion followed by heavy gunfire has been reported during a coordinated attack on a joint Army and Police checkpost in Manjhi, Dera Ismail Khan, KP Pakistan.

Local sources indicate a VBIED strike. The TTP has claimed responsibility, stating it launched a large scale assault on the checkpost.

Further details are awaited.

tweets.title: So admiral was confused between GE 404 and F414
tweets.content:
So admiral was confused between GE 404 and F414

chakranewz (@chakranewz)

We owe our audience a correction and an apology.

In a recent post promoting our conversation with Vice Admiral Raman Puri (Retd.), we incorrectly referred to the GE F404 engine. The reference should have been to the GE F414.

The error arose because the guest inadvertently misspoke during the conversation, and we failed to identify and correct it before publication. That is an editorial failure on our part.

We sincerely apologise for the mistake. Accuracy is fundamental to our work, and we regret that we fell short of that standard. We are reviewing our editorial processes and will endeavour to ensure that such errors do not occur again.

Thank you to those who pointed out the mistake and continue to hold us to high standards.

#KaveriEngine #IndianAirForce #Tejas #AMCA #DRDO #ChakraNewz
@SandeepUnnithan @awasthi_bob @i_DeepakBhadana

— https://nitter.net/chakranewz/status/2080297093550510335#m

tweets.title: India calls for "serious collective action against cyber scam centres" at East Asia Summit in Manila. Many Indian have been impacted by these scam centers in SE Asia
tweets.content:
India calls for "serious collective action against cyber scam centres" at East Asia Summit in Manila.

Many Indian have been impacted by these scam centers in SE Asia

Dr. S. Jaishankar (@DrSJaishankar)

Participated in the 21st East Asia Summit (EAS) in Manila today,making the points below in my statement:

✅ The global order is uncertain and volatile where interdependence and common interests co-exist with competitiveness and rivalries.

✅ There are structural changes being created by new capabilities, exposure and relationships; politics & security increasingly override economics and efficiency while the propensity to take risks and push the envelope is growing.

✅ India will be hosting two specific EAS activities this year - the 7th EAS Conference on Maritime Security Cooperation in Kochi and an EAS Maritime Heritage Festival in the ancient port city of Lothal.

✅ The world economy is today reeling under the impact of multiple conflicts; energy, food & health security cannot be taken for granted, especially by the Global South.

✅ Calling for dialogue and diplomacy to end the West Asia/Gulf conflict, India stresses that international waterways must remain safe & unimpeded, fully in consonance with international law. Under no circumstances can attacks on seafarers, civilian shipping or infrastructure be countenanced.

✅ On South China Sea, India supports the conclusion of a substantive, effective and legally binding CoC fully compliant with UNCLOS 1982 that does not prejudice the legitimate rights of all users.

✅ India stands for finding a political solution to the Myanmar issue; even as we continue to support the ASEAN efforts.

✅ Demand serious collective action against cyber scam centres, which have victimised Indian nationals in significant numbers.

✅ India has made notable relief and rehabilitation contributions and is a top emerging donor of UNRWA. We believe that a two-state solution can lead to a lasting and durable peace.

✅ Underlined India’s commitment to ASEAN centrality, regional stability and the realization of a free, open and prosperous Indo-Pacific.

— https://nitter.net/DrSJaishankar/status/2080226745630998560#m
tweets.author: @sidhant

tweets.title: RT by @alpha_defense: We owe our audience a correction and an apology. In a recent post promoting our conversation with Vice Admiral Raman Puri (Retd.), we incorrectly referred to the GE F404 engine. The reference should have been to the GE F414. The error arose because the guest inadvertently misspoke during the conversation, and we failed to identify and correct it before publication. That is an editorial failure on our part. We sincerely apologise for the mistake. Accuracy is fundamental to our work, and we regret that we fell short of that standard. We are reviewing our editorial processes and will endeavour to ensure that such errors do not occur again. Thank you to those who pointed out the mistake and continue to hold us to high standards. #KaveriEngine #IndianAirForce #Tejas #AMCA #DRDO #ChakraNewz @SandeepUnnithan @awasthi_bob @i_DeepakBhadana
tweets.content:
We owe our audience a correction and an apology.

In a recent post promoting our conversation with Vice Admiral Raman Puri (Retd.), we incorrectly referred to the GE F404 engine. The reference should have been to the GE F414.

The error arose because the guest inadvertently misspoke during the conversation, and we failed to identify and correct it before publication. That is an editorial failure on our part.

We sincerely apologise for the mistake. Accuracy is fundamental to our work, and we regret that we fell short of that standard. We are reviewing our editorial processes and will endeavour to ensure that such errors do not occur again.

Thank you to those who pointed out the mistake and continue to hold us to high standards.

#KaveriEngine #IndianAirForce #Tejas #AMCA #DRDO #ChakraNewz

tweets.title: RT by @sidhant: The Press Club of India condemns the recent attacks on and violence against journalists who are covering the youth protests in New Delhi. We have also seen with particular concern @Outlookindia Magazine's report that one of their journalists was fired upon by a weapon resembling a pellet gun and has severely injured his arm. At least three other such instances of pellet-like injuries have also been reported. We urge the police and security personnel, and those gathered to ensure their safety. We also call upon the government to restore mobile internet services in the area at this crucial time.
tweets.content:
The Press Club of India condemns the recent attacks on and violence against journalists who are covering the youth protests in New Delhi.

We have also seen with particular concern @Outlookindia Magazine's report that one of their journalists was fired upon by a weapon resembling a pellet gun and has severely injured his arm.

At least three other such instances of pellet-like injuries have also been reported.

We urge the police and security personnel, and those gathered to ensure their safety.

We also call upon the government to restore mobile internet services in the area at this crucial time.

Guest 2: “Americans, every bill is accounted for.” People ask, what are we getting in return for this mediation? If we are putting in so much effort, what are we getting back? tweets.content:
Pakistanis often reject the “beggar” label, but this discussion tells a different story.

Anchor: Pakistan has demanded a $10 billion brokerage fee from the US (for brokering a deal between the US and Iran)

Narendra Modi (@narendramodi)

Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth!

We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks. Have directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps in this regard. This continues our series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students.

Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared.

देश के युवाओं का हित और उनका भविष्य हमारी सर्वोच्च प्राथमिकता है।

पेपर लीक के सभी मामलों में दोषियों को जल्द से जल्द कठोर दंड देने के लिए, केस के त्वरित निस्तारण के लिए फास्ट ट्रैक कोर्ट्स का गठन किया जाएगा। इस संबंध में निर्देश दे दिए गए हैं कि संबंधित विभाग आवश्यक कार्रवाई सुनिश्चित करें।

विद्यार्थियों के हितों को सुरक्षित करने के लिए, सरकार द्वारा लिए जा रहे निर्णयों की श्रृंखला में ये एक महत्वपूर्ण कदम है।

युवाओं के भविष्य के साथ खिलवाड़ करने वाले किसी भी व्यक्ति को बख्शा नहीं जाएगा।

tweets.title: Most police forces in the country are overworked, underequipped, and outnumbered. Delhi Police is outnumbered when it comes to tackling a protest of 1 lakh+ people, which is now growing every day. The total strength of Delhi Police is around 83,000 personnel, responsible for policing 1,484 square kilometres. In peacetime, one Delhi Police personnel is outnumbered by approximately 259 civilians, let alone during a protest of this scale. A mob tends to develop a mentality to flank and attack when it sees security forces being outnumbered. To assert control when outnumbered, they have to use force. Additional reinforcement from the CRPF now seems necessary to control the situation, considering that everything is happening in a highly sensitive zone of the country.
tweets.content:
Most police forces in the country are overworked, underequipped, and outnumbered.

Delhi Police is outnumbered when it comes to tackling a protest of 1 lakh+ people, which is now growing every day. The total strength of Delhi Police is around 83,000 personnel, responsible for policing 1,484 square kilometres.

In peacetime, one Delhi Police personnel is outnumbered by approximately 259 civilians, let alone during a protest of this scale.

A mob tends to develop a mentality to flank and attack when it sees security forces being outnumbered.

To assert control when outnumbered, they have to use force.

Additional reinforcement from the CRPF now seems necessary to control the situation, considering that everything is happening in a highly sensitive zone of the country.

tweets.title: Apollo Micro Systems Limited (AMS) today announced that it has been awarded a Make-II Prototype Sanction Order (PSO) by the Indian Navy for the development of the SAVIOR-ASW the Semi- Submersible Autonomous Vessel for Intelligence, Operations and Reconnaissance. What is the SAVIOR-ASW System? SAVIOR-ASW is India's first indigenously developed semi-submersible autonomous vessel designed specifically for Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) surveillance. It is an unmanned, self-powered maritime platform, yet capable of patrolling the ocean independently for longer period. It operates silently beneath the sea surface, using a sophisticated hydrophone array to listen for submarines and underwater threats, and transmits intelligence continuously to Naval command centres ashore and aboard ships entirely without human presence onboard.
tweets.content:
Apollo Micro Systems Limited (AMS) today announced that it has been awarded a Make-II Prototype
Sanction Order (PSO) by the Indian Navy for the development of the SAVIOR-ASW the Semi-
Submersible Autonomous Vessel for Intelligence, Operations and Reconnaissance.

What is the SAVIOR-ASW System?
SAVIOR-ASW is India's first indigenously developed semi-submersible autonomous vessel designed specifically for Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) surveillance.

It is an unmanned, self-powered
maritime platform, yet capable of patrolling the ocean independently for longer period. It operates silently beneath the sea surface, using a sophisticated hydrophone array to listen for submarines and
underwater threats, and transmits intelligence continuously to Naval command centres ashore and
aboard ships entirely without human presence onboard.

tweets.title: RT by @alpha_defense: The @sssdefence Raptor in 300 BLK was made for the special forces community & it’s a beast. Ideal for CQB roles, it performs better than the 5.56x45 mm NATO in short barrel configurations. Inside 200 m, supersonic loads deliver the same energy transfer & barrier penetration as a 7.62x39 round. And it’s integrally suppressed because we don’t like loud goodbyes.
tweets.content:
The @sssdefence Raptor in 300 BLK was made for the special forces community & it’s a beast. Ideal for CQB roles, it performs better than the 5.56x45 mm NATO in short barrel configurations. Inside 200 m, supersonic loads deliver the same energy transfer & barrier penetration as a 7.62x39 round. And it’s integrally suppressed because we don’t like loud goodbyes.

SSS Defence (@sssdefence)

We've introduced our platforms before, but now, we want you to experience the engineering behind them.

Next up in the product showcase: the SSS Defence RAPTOR, chambered in .300 Blackout.

Enjoy the view.

#BorninBharat #300BLK

tweets.title: This will be the fourth time this government has failed to contain very manageable violence inside the country, that too in the national capital every single time. - The CAA/NRC protests turned violent without much ground. - Shaheen Bagh turned into the Delhi riots. - The farmers' protest turned into the most audacious dash up to the Red Fort. And now, this CJP protest. I keep seeing the same faces that I have seen before every violent protest. Nothing happens to anyone. This protest, violence, and anger among the youth will start costing the ruling party dearly in the coming times. But apart from the electoral impact, the sheer incompetence in managing very simple situations, maintaining positive optics, and keeping anarchist elements in check is astonishing. It is funny that managing these things is apparently so hard for the government that they end up beating students without effectively communicating to them what they are doing wrong. Good luck with this approach.
tweets.content:
This will be the fourth time this government has failed to contain very manageable violence inside the country, that too in the national capital every single time.

- The CAA/NRC protests turned violent without much ground.

- Shaheen Bagh turned into the Delhi riots.

- The farmers' protest turned into the most audacious dash up to the Red Fort.

And now, this CJP protest.

I keep seeing the same faces that I have seen before every violent protest. Nothing happens to anyone.

This protest, violence, and anger among the youth will start costing the ruling party dearly in the coming times.

But apart from the electoral impact, the sheer incompetence in managing very simple situations, maintaining positive optics, and keeping anarchist elements in check is astonishing.

It is funny that managing these things is apparently so hard for the government that they end up beating students without effectively communicating to them what they are doing wrong.

Good luck with this approach.
tweets.author: @elitepredatorss
